funFacts.fact: Australia has the world's largest sand island - Fraser Island, stretching 123km.
The Great Barrier Reef is visible from space and is the world's largest living structure.
Australia has more than 10,000 beaches - you could visit a new one every day for 27 years!
The Australian Alps receive more snow than Switzerland.
Perth is the most isolated capital city in the world, closer to Singapore than Sydney.
Australia has the most venomous snake in the world - the Inland Taipan.
There are 60 wine regions in Australia across the country.
The Australian dollar is made from polymer (plastic) and was the first plastic currency in the world.
The Sydney Opera House roof weighs over 161,000 tonnes - equivalent to 32,000 elephants.
Australians invented Wi-Fi, the black box flight recorder, and the bionic ear.
Australia has the world's largest population of wild camels - over 1 million!
The Great Ocean Road is the world's largest war memorial, built by returned soldiers.
Lake Hillier is a naturally pink lake - scientists still aren't 100% sure why.
Australia has 19 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
The Box Jellyfish has 24 eyes and is one of the most venomous creatures on Earth.
Australia was the second country in the world to give women the right to vote (1902).
The platypus is so unusual that British scientists thought it was a hoax when first discovered.
Australia has over 750 reptile species - more than any other country.
The world's oldest fossil, dating back 3.4 billion years, was found in Australia.
Tasmania has the cleanest air in the world.
The Australian War Memorial is one of the world's great museums.
Australia is the only continent without an active volcano.
The Aussie 'selfie' was invented in Australia in 2002.
There are more kangaroos in Australia than people (about 50 million vs 26 million).
The world's longest fence is in Australia - the Dingo Fence stretches 5,614km.
Uluru changes colour throughout the day, from ochre to burning red to deep purple.
Australia's national anthem was 'God Save the Queen' until 1984.
The MCG (Melbourne Cricket Ground) can hold over 100,000 people.
Australia has the most public libraries per capita in the world.
Vegemite was invented in 1922 and 22 million jars are sold each year.
The Australian Capital Territory was specifically designed by Walter Burley Griffin.
Australia is home to the world's largest monolith - Uluru.
The emu and kangaroo appear on the coat of arms because they can't walk backwards easily.
Australia has 8,222 islands within its maritime borders.
Woolloomooloo has the longest place name in Australia with consecutive double letters.
Australia has the world's smallest penguin species - the Little Penguin (33cm tall).
The Great Barrier Reef has over 1,500 fish species and 400 types of coral.
Australia invented the notepad, the wine cask, and the inflatable aircraft slide.
The Melbourne Cup is known as 'the race that stops the nation'.
Australia is the world's largest exporter of beef and wool.
Perth is the sunniest capital city in Australia with an average of 8.8 hours of sunshine daily.
The Australian $50 note has a typo - 'responsibility' was spelled wrong (fixed in 2018).
The Torres Strait has 274 islands.
AFL (Aussie Rules Football) was originally invented to keep cricketers fit in winter.
Australia has over 1,500 species of spiders - but only a few are dangerous to humans.
The Sydney Harbour Bridge is affectionately known as 'The Coathanger'.
Australia's first police force was made up of 12 well-behaved convicts.
The wombat's poop is cube-shaped to stop it rolling away.
Australia is one of the few countries where eating its national animal (kangaroo) is legal.
The Australian outback covers about 70% of the continent.
Tasmania was once connected to mainland Australia before sea levels rose.
The Opera House was designed by Danish architect Jørn Utzon who never saw the finished building.
Australia has the world's largest cattle station - Anna Creek Station (6,000,000 acres).
The echidna and platypus are the only egg-laying mammals (monotremes) in the world.
Australia's cattle population is about 28 million - more than the human population.
The Nullarbor Plain has the longest stretch of straight railway track in the world (478km).
Australians consume 1.7 billion meat pies per year.
The Australian accent developed from the mixture of British and Irish settlers.
Australia was the first country to use banknotes made of polymer.
The kookaburra's laugh is used in movies as a jungle sound effect.
The Great Barrier Reef contributes over $6 billion to the Australian economy annually.
Australia has participated in every modern Olympic Games since 1896.
Fraser Island has rainforests growing on sand dunes at elevations over 200 metres.
Australia is the only country that eats its national emblem (kangaroo).
The lyrebird can mimic almost any sound, including chainsaws and car alarms.
Australia is the sixth largest country in the world by area.
The Great Barrier Reef is about 500,000 years old.
Australia has the highest rate of skin cancer in the world.
The kangaroo population can double in good seasons, reaching up to 60 million.
Australia is home to the world's largest crocodiles - the Saltwater Crocodile.
The first European settlers in Australia drank more alcohol per person than any other time in history.
Australia's Highway 1 is the longest national highway in the world (14,500 km).
The Quokka is known as the world's happiest animal and only lives on Rottnest Island.
Australia has 16 world heritage listed sites.
The eucalyptus tree is native only to Australia.
A koala sleeps up to 22 hours a day.
The Tasmanian Devil is the largest carnivorous marsupial in the world.
Australia's Parliament House in Canberra has over 4,500 rooms.
The Australian government created the Royal Flying Doctor Service in 1928.
Australia has the longest railway crossing in the world on the Nullarbor Plain (478km).
The Great Ocean Road took 13 years to build by hand by returned soldiers.
Australians invented the wine cask (goon bag) in 1965.
The didgeridoo is over 1,500 years old and is the world's oldest wind instrument.
Australia is the only country-continent in the world.
Shark Bay in Western Australia has stromatolites that are 3.5 billion years old.
Australia Day marks the arrival of the First Fleet, but Indigenous Australians call it Invasion Day.
The word 'kangaroo' comes from the Guugu Yimithirr language (gangurru).
Australians call McDonald's 'Maccas' and it's officially on the menu.
Australia has the longest straight section of railway in the world - 478km across the Nullarbor.
The Great Barrier Reef is the size of 70 million football fields.
The drop bear is a mythical creature invented to scare tourists, but koalas aren't actually dangerous.
Australia's first settlement was a penal colony for British convicts.
The Aboriginal flag was designed in 1971 by Harold Thomas, an Aboriginal artist.
Australia's population is extremely urbanised - 85% live within 50km of the coast.
The Sydney to Hobart yacht race is one of the toughest in the world.
Australia is the largest producer of opals in the world (95% of supply).
The first Australian of the Year award was given in 1960.
Canberra means 'meeting place' in the local Indigenous language.
Australia is the world's driest inhabited continent.
Australia has over 60 wine regions and produces over 1 billion litres of wine annually.
